explanation:
Responsible **pet care** follows international veterinary guidelines (WSAVA, AAHA). Nutrition, vaccination, and parasite control have protocols specific to species, breed, weight, and age.

## How It's Calculated

Rule: 1cm adult fish requires 1L water (cichlids and some species need more). This calculator applies the formula with your values, checking typical ranges and alerting you when values fall outside reasonable limits.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (medical, financial, or legal), consult with a qualified specialist. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much water does a fish need?
faq.a: The standard rule is 1cm of adult fish length requires 1L of water. Larger species like cichlids need more space—typically 1cm per 2-3L. Always provide adequate filtration and water circulation.

faq.q: What's the 1 inch per gallon rule?
faq.a: This is the imperial equivalent of the 1cm per 1L rule. One inch (2.54cm) of fish needs about 3.8L (1 gallon). It's a baseline for beginners; some species need significantly more.

faq.q: Do different fish species need different tank sizes?
faq.a: Yes. While the rule applies to most community fish, aggressive species (cichlids, pike), large fish, and those that patrol require more territory. Always research your specific species.

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate for my tank?
faq.a: It's a reference tool using standard guidelines. For best results, combine it with species-specific research, consult aquarium specialists, and monitor fish behavior. Overcrowding causes stress and health problems.

faq.q: Can I keep different fish species together?
faq.a: Some species are compatible, others aren't. Consider temperament, size, water parameters (pH, temperature), and tank territory. Mixing aggressive and peaceful fish typically fails.

faq.q: Do I need a filter for my aquarium?
faq.a: Yes. A filter removes waste and maintains water quality. Tank size, fish load, and bioload determine the filter strength needed. Most tanks require at least 3-4x hourly water turnover.

faq.q: How often should I change my tank water?
faq.a: For established tanks with proper filtration, 20-30% weekly changes maintain water quality. Heavily stocked tanks may need more frequent changes. Test water parameters to guide your routine.

faq.q: Is this calculator suitable for saltwater aquariums?
faq.a: The basic volume rule applies, but saltwater systems have different requirements (specific gravity, salinity, lighting). Consult saltwater-specific resources for accurate planning.

faq.q: Are your calculations free and private?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are free with no registration required. Calculations run in your browser—we never send your data to any server.
